1.1: OFFER THE ROSTER FIRST — read roster:brackenfoot and lay its roles before the player: the old campaigner (steel), the quick tongue (talk), the woodsman (the back-ways), the hedge-wife (mercy) — a party's natural spread and Brackenfoot's four ways through. The player may ADOPT one (fastest, and steadiest for a first sitting) or SET THEM ASIDE and build their own. A role is a template, not a name: whichever they take, the NAME is the handle they entered with — the role gives capability, want, look and knowings, the player gives the person.
1.2: CALIBRATE capability in the reference system's own terms — read rules:nomad (the bands: CF 1–20, a seasoned operator ~8, one peak, one weak spot). An adopted role already sits calibrated; copy its 1 (capability), 2 (want), 3 (appearance), 4 (knowings) as the character's, and let the player colour them. Building fresh: draw out one honest peak and one real weak spot, one sentence of want, how they look to a stranger, and at most two or three names or places carried from before (the knows seed — keep it thin).
1.3: NAME THE ARRIVAL — the road delivers a newcomer to the Slip, the ford where the good road gives out and Brackenfoot's track begins (spatial:brackenfoot, address 211, digit-path 2,1,1). This is where a stranger first meets the occupation: stopped at the rope, looked over, warned off or waved through. A party arriving together lands here as one — the first walker arrives at 211, and every later companion gives that first walker's HANDLE at the interview and lands at the same crossing (pscale:char-creation:4). Genesis founds no new places; the Slip is the door.
1._: THE INTERVIEW at Brackenfoot — the only branch this world changes from the canonical passage. Run it WITH the player where a human sits beside you; where none does (a rig or an LLM-app seat driving this handle), YOU are the player — pick or adopt a person you would enjoy, answer in one pass, and do not stall for a human who is not there. The handle the player entered with IS the character's name, settled at the door and unchangeable here (a companion joining gives that handle to find them).

content.2: THE WRITES — follow the canonical ceremony exactly: read bsp(agent_id='pscale', block='char-creation', spindle='2') and do as it says. In brief: the character becomes four blocks at THIS beach, each with its own underscore. passport:<handle> — _: the character in one zeroth-voice line (name, what they are, how they carry themselves); 1: capability (an adopted role's 1, or the built one), in rules:nomad terms; 2: the want; 3: the appearance sentence, then ' Location: *:<beach-url>:spatial:brackenfoot:211' (the Slip, digit-path copied plain, never dotted). knows:<handle> — {1: people, 2: places} (the role's 4, or the built seed). purpose:<handle> — the want, one line at the underscore. All three CREATED LOCKED under a passphrase the player chooses (new_lock at birth; never echo it back). witnessed:<handle> — the ARRIVAL MEMORY as the first entry (append=true creates it open), then a SECOND call locks it (new_lock, no secret needed). Write nothing else; the rest is earned in play.
content.3: THE ARRIVAL — follow bsp(agent_id='pscale', block='char-creation', spindle='3'). Re-enter with pscale_play(world, handle): the envelope now knows the character and hands the room at the Slip (211), its directive and live scene inlined. Commit an ARRIVING-BEAT to that room's pool — the public fact of a stranger crossing the ford, rendered by APPEARANCE (the name is not public until spoken in the fiction). From that beat the operating directive (pscale:grit) carries the turn: perceive, act, commit. The character is no longer new; Brackenfoot has one more living hand at its door.
content._: GENESIS at Brackenfoot — a world-hosted delta over the substrate passage (bsp(agent_id='pscale', block='char-creation')). Walked ONCE per handle: from no-blocks to a living, locked, placed character at the Slip. This world changes only the INTERVIEW (branch 1): it offers a ROSTER of ready roles to adopt (roster:brackenfoot), and it names the ARRIVAL (the Slip, 211). The writes (2) and the arrival (3) are the canonical ceremony, unchanged — read them at the sentinel and follow them exactly. Brackenfoot is a hard, grounded holding with no magic shown; a character is a person who could stand in a frightened village and be counted, not a hero. Creation is a hatching, not a signup.
